front: Work: a bet between two gambling addicts to transport a glass church across New South Wales
Work: Theophilus Hopkins beats his son for eating Christmas pudding, inspired by Edmund Gosse's Father and Son
Work: an Anglican priest and an industrial heiress named Lucinda Leplastrier are the title characters
Work: one title character drowns after delivering a glass church to Reverend Hassett in Bellingen
Work: inspired by the Ern Malley hoax; Christopher Chubb creates the fictional poet Bob McCorkle
back: My Life as a Fake (Peter Carey)

front: Work: Bob McCorkle is a seven-foot giant dressed in black; Chubb runs a bicycle shop in Kuala Lumpur
back: My Life as a Fake (Peter Carey)

front: Work: narrated by Sarah Wode-Douglass; Chubb's literary creation may have kidnapped his daughter Tina
back: My Life as a Fake (Peter Carey)

front: Work: narrated by a 139-year-old con man named Herbert Badgery
Work: Goon Tse Ying teaches the protagonist the art of disappearance; a dragon appears each time
Work: ends with Hissao accidentally killing a rare bird he is smuggling
Work: Phoebe runs away to continue her affair with Annette Davidson after Jack McGrath marries her off to the protagonist
Work: the title outlaw leads a band who construct homemade armor in the Australian outback
back: True History of the Kelly Gang (Peter Carey)
work: True History of the Kelly Gang

front: Work: drew heavily on the style of the Jerilderie Letter; the title character is betrayed by Thomas Curnow before a shootout in Glenrowan
back: True History of the Kelly Gang (Peter Carey)
work: True History of the Kelly Gang

front: Work: a reworking of Great Expectations from Abel Magwitch's perspective; Dickens is fictionalized as Tobias Oates
Work: Catherine assembles a mechanical duck revealed to be a swan; a character mourns her colleague Matthew while discovering Henry Brandling's diaries
Author: won the Miles Franklin Award three times, for Bliss, Jack Maggs, and Oscar and Lucinda
Author: considered perhaps Australia's best candidate for the Nobel Prize in Literature
